logs.content:
/a0/usr/workdir/gchat-output.md 217 lines
>>>
  1 
  2 
  3 ## [1/54] DPD x Platform Engineering
  4 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAcjNXKpA/5M_Se2cruRU | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-13T02:56:15.169000+00:00 | Work Items: marketing-service, orchestrator | People: Maou Sheng Lee, Daryl Ng, Michael Bui, Kyle Nguyen, Jazz Tong | Labels: log-optimization, production-incident, cost-reduction, service-governance, engineering-leadership
  5 In a Google Chat discussion regarding unexpected Datadog logging spikes in the production environment starting March 29, Maou Sheng Lee flagged high costs associated with services including marketing-service and orchestrator. Michael Bui was explicitly @mentioned and confirmed ownership of the issue, identifying it as related to dynamic slots in swimlanes and infinite scrolling features within the marketing service. He committed to addressing the root cause and optimizing log volume within 1-2 days. The conversation involved cross-team collaboration with Kyle Nguyen and Jazz Tong, confirming that while the team is investigating, Michael's intervention is required to resolve the production reliability and cost optimization issue.
  6 
  7 ## [2/54] Nikhil Grover
  8 Source: gchat | Group: dm/t3wf6EAAAAE/u6SLGnAyeCA | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-13T03:58:20.625000+00:00 | Work Items: LIGHT-394 | People: Nikhil Grover, Michael Bui, Vera | Labels: ad-tech-architecture, inventory-scoping, data-contract-review, page-targeting, osmos-integration
  9 Nikhil Grover engaged Michael Bui in a direct architecture review for the floating icon feature (LIGHT-394), resolving ambiguity on ad targeting scope. The discussion confirmed the UI component is global but backend inventory must be page-specific to prevent cross-brand conflicts (e.g., Pepsi ads on Coke campaign pages). Nikhil clarified that Osmos will manage separate inventories per page type, requiring frontend apps to pass specific Page IDs and types in ad requests. Michael validated this approach as it avoids the management complexity seen in previous interstitial implementations where shared units lacked context. While Michael temporarily deferred deep analysis of the full epic due to current priorities, he confirmed the data contract direction is acceptable pending final verification.
 10 
 11 ## [3/54] Hengky Sucanda
 12 Source: gchat | Group: dm/_gwJCgAAAAE | Relevance: 8/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-13T04:07:16.540000+00:00 | Work Items: LIGHT-32, PR #20 | People: Michael Bui, Hengky Sucanda | Labels: ai-reliability, tool-assessment, light-project, engineering-discussion, budget-pending

Hengky confirmed no current experience with such degradation. 211 ## [53/54] DPD x Platform Engineering
212 Source: gchat | Group: space/AAQAcjNXKpA/6XSKrbG2aIM | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-16T14:18:53.759000+00:00 | People: Michael Bui, Maou Sheng Lee, Daryl Ng, Dang Hung Cuong, Kyle Nguyen | Labels: redis-incident, memory-leak, cache-troubleshooting, gcp-monitoring, root-cause-analysis
213 On April 16, 2026, Michael Bui led a troubleshooting session for a Redis cache (zs-fpon-prd) memory spike reaching 90%. While initial speculation linked the issue to recent repository changes in seller services, Michael clarified that his reviewed code for product ad position updates does not utilize Redis. By analyzing Datadog graphs spanning the past year, he identified that memory usage has been increasing over time with intermittent pauses rather than a steady rise, pointing towards a potential cache leak or accumulation of keys without Time-To-Live (TTL) settings. Michael hypothesized that 'None-TTL' keys in db2 are the root cause and linked the issue to specific code in `sync-capacity-utilization/main.go`. He engaged Daryl Ng, Akash Gupta, Harry Akbar Ali Munir, Maou Sheng Lee, Kyle Nguyen, and Dang Hung Cuong to investigate further. The group agreed that while they have 1-2 months of buffer before capacity issues become critical, a Jira ticket needs to be created to track the investigation and potential Redis size increase is being considered as a temporary mitigation.
214 
215 ## [54/54] Nikhil Grover
216 Source: gchat | Group: dm/t3wf6EAAAAE | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-16T14:19:41.716000+00:00 | Work Items: LIGHT-34, LIGHT-394, LIGHT-391 | People: Nikhil Grover, Vera, Koklin, Michael Bui | Labels: retail-media, api-contracting, event-tracking, architectural-review, video-advertising
217 Michael Bui is directly engaged in a high-priority architectural discussion with Nikhil Grover regarding Project LIGHT's retail media listing widgets. The conversation focuses on resolving data query flows for impression tracking (OSMOS vs Algolia integration), clarifying 'ad_format' field usage, and aligning on store data sources (FFS stores in DBP). Michael identified a need to leave an upcoming meeting early to prioritize this technical alignment. Key implementation gaps were discussed, including PLP page definitions (search vs category) and ad unit mapping for 'For You' and 'Past Purchase' tabs. Nikhil shared an Ad inventory mapping spreadsheet and event tracking story (LIGHT-34) for review. Michael provided detailed feedback on the tracking story to ensure developers have sufficient details, specifically regarding video events which lack pre-constructed URLs like impression/click events. He clarified that Frontend must contract the event object and send it to the OSMOS generic tracking API for video actions and view percentages. To improve clarity, Michael updated the Ad Service API Contract on Confluence and suggested explicitly adding event parameters as expectations in the documentation. The team initiated a Google Meet call to resolve these user story gaps before proceeding.
